summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Naija LMS MVP - Nigerian Learning Management System. Backend APIs working well (91.7% success rate), frontend loads with proper Nigerian-inspired design, authentication flow functional, but critical UI overlay issue blocking button interactions on homepage.

minor_issues.issue: Cannot test single course endpoint due to no sample courses in database
minor_issues.impact: Testing limitation only - endpoint works when courses exist

critical_bugs.issue: Button clicks intercepted by overlay elements - CTA buttons not clickable
critical_bugs.details: Overlay divs with absolute positioning blocking pointer events on hero CTA buttons

ui_bugs.issue: Logout button not easily discoverable in user interface

design_issues.issue: Nigerian-inspired warm orange/gold design implemented correctly

passed_tests: Get current user endpoint (returns user data)
Get all courses endpoint (returns empty array correctly)
Contact form submission (API accepts form data)
User enrollments endpoint (returns empty array for new user)
User payments endpoint (returns empty array for new user)
Admin access control (properly blocks non-admin users)
Homepage loads with proper Nigerian design colors
Courses page loads and search functionality works
Registration page loads with all form fields
Login page loads with proper form elements
About page loads with comprehensive content
Contact page loads with functional contact form
Navigation between pages works via direct URLs
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix homepage hero section overlay issue preventing CTA button clicks. The overlay divs are intercepting pointer events on the Browse Courses and Get Started buttons. Also improve logout button visibility in student dashboard. Consider adding sample courses to database for better user experience.
